Use the \"external storage\" mode to prevent\n     * OpenSSL from creating an internal session cache.\n     */\n    SSL_CTX_set_session_cache_mode(ctx->ossl.ssl_ctx,\n                                   SSL_SESS_CACHE_CLIENT |\n                                   SSL_SESS_CACHE_NO_INTERNAL);\n    SSL_CTX_sess_set_new_cb(ctx->ossl.ssl_ctx, quic_ossl_new_session_cb);\n  }\n\n#elif defined(USE_GNUTLS)\n  if(ngtcp2_crypto_gnutls_configure_client_session(ctx->gtls.session) != 0) {\n    failf(data, \"ngtcp2_crypto_gnutls_configure_client_session failed\");\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n  if(Curl_ssl_scache_use(cf, data)) {\n    gnutls_handshake_set_hook_function(ctx->gtls.session,\n                                       GNUTLS_HANDSHAKE_ANY, GNUTLS_HOOK_POST,\n                                       quic_gtls_handshake_cb);\n  }\n\n#elif defined(USE_WOLFSSL)\n  if(ngtcp2_crypto_wolfssl_configure_client_context(ctx->wssl.ssl_ctx) != 0) {\n    failf(data, \"ngtcp2_crypto_wolfssl_configure_client_context failed\");\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n  if(Curl_ssl_scache_use(cf, data)) {\n    /* Register to get notified when a new session is received */\n    wolfSSL_CTX_sess_set_new_cb(ctx->wssl.ssl_ctx, wssl_quic_new_session_cb);\n  }\n#endif\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_on_session_reuse(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                           struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                           struct alpn_spec *alpns,\n                                           struct Curl_ssl_session *scs,\n                                           bool *do_early_data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  *do_early_data = FALSE;\n#if defined(USE_OPENSSL) && defined(HAVE_OPENSSL_EARLYDATA)\n  ctx->earlydata_max = scs->earlydata_max;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_GNUTLS\n  ctx->earlydata_max =\n    gnutls_record_get_max_early_data_size(ctx->tls.gtls.session);\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_WOLFSSL\n#ifdef WOLFSSL_EARLY_DATA\n  ctx->earlydata_max = scs->earlydata_max;\n#else\n  ctx->earlydata_max = 0;\n#endif /* WOLFSSL_EARLY_DATA */\n#endif\n#if defined(USE_GNUTLS) || defined(USE_WOLFSSL) || \\\n  (defined(USE_OPENSSL) && defined(HAVE_OPENSSL_EARLYDATA))\n  if(!ctx->earlydata_max) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"SSL session does not allow earlydata\");\n  }\n  else if(!Curl_alpn_contains_proto(alpns, scs->alpn)) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"SSL session from different ALPN, no early data\");\n  }\n  else if(!scs->quic_tp || !scs->quic_tp_len) {\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"no 0RTT transport parameters, no early data, \");\n  }\n  else {\n    int rv;\n    rv = ngtcp2_conn_decode_and_set_0rtt_transport_params(\n      ctx->qconn, (const uint8_t *)scs->quic_tp, scs->quic_tp_len);\n    if(rv)\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"no early data, failed to set 0RTT transport \"\n                  \"parameters: %s\", ngtcp2_strerror(rv));\n    else {\n      infof(data, \"SSL session allows %zu bytes of early data, \"\n            \"reusing ALPN '%s'\", ctx->earlydata_max, scs->alpn);\n      result = init_ngh3_conn(cf, data);\n      if(!result) {\n        ctx->use_earlydata = TRUE;\n        cf->connected = TRUE;\n        *do_early_data = TRUE;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n#else /* not supported in the TLS backend */\n  (void)data;\n  (void)ctx;\n  (void)scs;\n  (void)alpns;\n#endif\n  return result;\n}\n\n/*\n * Might be called twice for happy eyeballs.\n */\nstatic CURLcode cf_connect_start(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                 struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                 struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  int rc;\n  int rv;\n  CURLcode result;\n  const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *sockaddr = NULL;\n  int qfd;\n  static const struct alpn_spec ALPN_SPEC_H3 = { { \"h3\", \"h3-29\" }, 2 };\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(ctx->initialized);\n  ctx->dcid.datalen = NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN;\n  result = Curl_rand(data, ctx->dcid.data, NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  ctx->scid.datalen = NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN;\n  result = Curl_rand(data, ctx->scid.data, NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  (void)Curl_qlogdir(data, ctx->scid.data, NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN, &qfd);\n  ctx->qlogfd = qfd; /* -1 if failure above */\n  quic_settings(ctx, data, pktx);\n\n  result = vquic_ctx_init(data, &ctx->q);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  if(Curl_cf_socket_peek(cf->next, data, &ctx->q.sockfd, &sockaddr, NULL))\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  ctx->q.local_addrlen = sizeof(ctx->q.local_addr);\n  rv = getsockname(ctx->q.sockfd, (struct sockaddr *)&ctx->q.local_addr,\n                   &ctx->q.local_addrlen);\n  if(rv == -1)\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n\n  ngtcp2_addr_init(&ctx->connected_path.local,\n                   (struct sockaddr *)&ctx->q.local_addr,\n                   ctx->q.local_addrlen);\n  ngtcp2_addr_init(&ctx->connected_path.remote,\n                   &sockaddr->curl_sa_addr, (socklen_t)sockaddr->addrlen);\n\n  rc = ngtcp2_conn_client_new(&ctx->qconn, &ctx->dcid, &ctx->scid,\n                              &ctx->connected_path,\n                              NGTCP2_PROTO_VER_V1, &ng_callbacks,\n                              &ctx->settings, &ctx->transport_params,\n                              Curl_ngtcp2_mem(), cf);\n  if(rc)\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n\n  ctx->conn_ref.get_conn = get_conn;\n  ctx->conn_ref.user_data = cf;\n\n  result = Curl_vquic_tls_init(&ctx->tls, cf, data, &ctx->peer, &ALPN_SPEC_H3,\n                               cf_ngtcp2_tls_ctx_setup, &ctx->tls,\n                               &ctx->conn_ref,\n                               cf_ngtcp2_on_session_reuse);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n#if defined(USE_OPENSSL) && defined(OPENSSL_QUIC_API2)\n  if(ngtcp2_crypto_ossl_ctx_new(&ctx->ossl_ctx, ctx->tls.ossl.ssl) != 0) {\n    failf(data, \"ngtcp2_crypto_ossl_ctx_new failed\");\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n  ngtcp2_conn_set_tls_native_handle(ctx->qconn, ctx->ossl_ctx);\n  if(ngtcp2_crypto_ossl_configure_client_session(ctx->tls.ossl.ssl) != 0) {\n    failf(data, \"ngtcp2_crypto_ossl_configure_client_session failed\");\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n#elif defined(USE_OPENSSL)\n  SSL_set_quic_use_legacy_codepoint(ctx->tls.ossl.ssl, 0);\n  ngtcp2_conn_set_tls_native_handle(ctx->qconn, ctx->tls.ossl.ssl);\n#elif defined(USE_GNUTLS)\n  ngtcp2_conn_set_tls_native_handle(ctx->qconn, ctx->tls.gtls.session);\n#elif defined(USE_WOLFSSL)\n  ngtcp2_conn_set_tls_native_handle(ctx->qconn, ctx->tls.wssl.ssl);\n#else\n#error \"ngtcp2 TLS backend not defined\"\n#endif\n\n  ngtcp2_ccerr_default(&ctx->last_error);\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_connect(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                  struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                  bool *done)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx pktx;\n\n  if(cf->connected) {\n    *done = TRUE;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  /* Connect the UDP filter first */\n  if(!cf->next->connected) {\n    result = Curl_conn_cf_connect(cf->next, data, done);\n    if(result || !*done)\n      return result;\n  }\n\n  *done = FALSE;\n  pktx_init(&pktx, cf, data);\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n\n  if(!ctx->qconn) {\n    ctx->started_at = *Curl_pgrs_now(data);\n    result = cf_connect_start(cf, data, &pktx);\n    if(result)\n      goto out;\n    if(cf->connected) {\n      *done = TRUE;\n      goto out;\n    }\n    result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, &pktx);\n    /* we do not expect to be able to recv anything yet */\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  result = cf_progress_ingress(cf, data, &pktx);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, &pktx);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  if(ngtcp2_conn_get_handshake_completed(ctx->qconn)) {\n    result = ctx->tls_vrfy_result;\n    if(!result) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"peer verified\");\n      cf->connected = TRUE;\n      *done = TRUE;\n    }\n  }\n\nout:\n  if(ctx->qconn &&\n     ((result == CURLE_RECV_ERROR) || (result == CURLE_SEND_ERROR)) &&\n     ngtcp2_conn_in_draining_period(ctx->qconn)) {\n    const ngtcp2_ccerr *cerr = ngtcp2_conn_get_ccerr(ctx->qconn);\n\n    result = CURLE_COULDNT_CONNECT;\n    if(cerr) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"connect error, type=%d, code=%\" PRIu64,\n                  cerr->type, cerr->error_code);\n      switch(cerr->type) {\n      case NGTCP2_CCERR_TYPE_VERSION_NEGOTIATION:\n        CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"error in version negotiation\");\n        break;\n      default:\n        if(cerr->error_code >= NGTCP2_CRYPTO_ERROR) {\n          CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"crypto error, tls alert=%u\",\n                      (unsigned int)(cerr->error_code & 0xffU));\n        }\n        else if(cerr->error_code == NGTCP2_CONNECTION_REFUSED) {\n          CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"connection refused by server\");\n          /* When a QUIC server instance is shutting down, it may send us a\n           * CONNECTION_CLOSE with this code right away. We want\n           * to keep on trying in this case. */\n          result = CURLE_WEIRD_SERVER_REPLY;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n#ifdef CURLVERBOSE\n  if(result) {\n    struct ip_quadruple ip;\n\n    if(!Curl_cf_socket_peek(cf->next, data, NULL, NULL, &ip))\n      infof(data, \"QUIC connect to %s port %u failed: %s\",\n            ip.remote_ip, ip.remote_port, curl_easy_strerror(result));\n  }\n#endif\n  if(!result && ctx->qconn) {\n    result = check_and_set_expiry(cf, data, &pktx);\n  }\n  if(result || *done)\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"connect -> %d, done=%d\", result, *done);\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_query(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                int query, int *pres1, void *pres2)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  switch(query) {\n  case CF_QUERY_MAX_CONCURRENT: {\n    DEBUGASSERT(pres1);\n    CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n    /* Set after transport params arrived and continually updated\n     * by callback. QUIC counts the number over the lifetime of the\n     * connection, ever increasing.\n     * We count the *open* transfers plus the budget for new ones. */\n    if(!ctx->qconn || ctx->shutdown_started) {\n      *pres1 = 0;\n    }\n    else if(ctx->max_bidi_streams) {\n      uint64_t avail_bidi_streams = 0;\n      uint64_t max_streams = cf->conn->attached_xfers;\n      if(ctx->max_bidi_streams > ctx->used_bidi_streams)\n        avail_bidi_streams = ctx->max_bidi_streams - ctx->used_bidi_streams;\n      max_streams += avail_bidi_streams;\n      *pres1 = (max_streams > INT_MAX) ? INT_MAX : (int)max_streams;\n    }\n    else  /* transport params not arrived yet? take our default. */\n      *pres1 = (int)Curl_multi_max_concurrent_streams(data->multi);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"query conn[%\" FMT_OFF_T \"]: \"\n                \"MAX_CONCURRENT -> %d (%u in use)\",\n                cf->conn->connection_id, *pres1, cf->conn->attached_xfers);\n    CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n  case CF_QUERY_CONNECT_REPLY_MS:\n    if(ctx->q.got_first_byte) {\n      timediff_t ms = curlx_ptimediff_ms(&ctx->q.first_byte_at,\n                                         &ctx->started_at);\n      *pres1 = (ms < INT_MAX) ? (int)ms : INT_MAX;\n    }\n    else\n      *pres1 = -1;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  case CF_QUERY_TIMER_CONNECT: {\n    struct curltime *when = pres2;\n    if(ctx->q.got_first_byte)\n      *when = ctx->q.first_byte_at;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n  case CF_QUERY_TIMER_APPCONNECT: {\n    struct curltime *when = pres2;\n    if(cf->connected)\n      *when = ctx->handshake_at;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n  case CF_QUERY_HTTP_VERSION:\n    *pres1 = 30;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  case CF_QUERY_SSL_INFO:\n  case CF_QUERY_SSL_CTX_INFO: {\n    struct curl_tlssessioninfo *info = pres2;\n    if(Curl_vquic_tls_get_ssl_info(&ctx->tls,\n                                   (query == CF_QUERY_SSL_CTX_INFO), info))\n      return CURLE_OK;\n    break;\n  }\n  case CF_QUERY_ALPN_NEGOTIATED: {\n    const char **palpn = pres2;\n    DEBUGASSERT(palpn);\n    *palpn = cf->connected ? \"h3\" : NULL;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n  default:\n    break;\n  }\n  return cf->next ?\n    cf->next->cft->query(cf->next, data, query, pres1, pres2) :\n    CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n}\n\nstatic bool cf_ngtcp2_conn_is_alive(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                    struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                    bool *input_pending)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  bool alive = FALSE;\n  const ngtcp2_transport_params *rp;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  *input_pending = FALSE;\n  if(!ctx->qconn || ctx->shutdown_started)\n    goto out;\n\n  /* We do not announce a max idle timeout, but when the peer does\n   * it will close the connection when it expires. */\n  rp = ngtcp2_conn_get_remote_transport_params(ctx->qconn);\n  if(rp && rp->max_idle_timeout) {\n    timediff_t idletime_ms =\n      curlx_ptimediff_ms(Curl_pgrs_now(data), &ctx->q.last_io);\n    if(idletime_ms > 0) {\n      uint64_t max_idle_ms =\n        (uint64_t)(rp->max_idle_timeout / NGTCP2_MILLISECONDS);\n      if((uint64_t)idletime_ms > max_idle_ms)\n        goto out;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(!cf->next || !cf->next->cft->is_alive(cf->next, data, input_pending))\n    goto out;\n\n  alive = TRUE;\n  if(*input_pending) {\n    CURLcode result;\n    /* This happens before we have sent off a request and the connection is\n       not in use by any other transfer, there should not be any data here,\n       only \"protocol frames\" */\n    *input_pending = FALSE;\n    result = cf_progress_ingress(cf, data, NULL);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"is_alive, progress ingress -> %d\", result);\n    alive = result ? FALSE : TRUE;\n  }\n\nout:\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return alive;\n}\n\nstruct Curl_cftype Curl_cft_http3 = {\n  \"HTTP/3\",\n  CF_TYPE_IP_CONNECT | CF_TYPE_SSL | CF_TYPE_MULTIPLEX | CF_TYPE_HTTP,\n  0,\n  cf_ngtcp2_destroy,\n  cf_ngtcp2_connect,\n  cf_ngtcp2_close,\n  cf_ngtcp2_shutdown,\n  cf_ngtcp2_adjust_pollset,\n  Curl_cf_def_data_pending,\n  cf_ngtcp2_send,\n  cf_ngtcp2_recv,\n  cf_ngtcp2_cntrl,\n  cf_ngtcp2_conn_is_alive,\n  Curl_cf_def_conn_keep_alive,\n  cf_ngtcp2_query,\n};\n\nCURLcode Curl_cf_ngtcp2_create(struct Curl_cfilter **pcf,\n                               struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct connectdata *conn,\n                               const struct Curl_addrinfo *ai)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = NULL;\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = NULL;\n  CURLcode result;\n\n  ctx = curlx_calloc(1, sizeof(*ctx));\n  if(!ctx) {\n    result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    goto out;\n  }\n  cf_ngtcp2_ctx_init(ctx);\n\n  result = Curl_cf_create(&cf, &Curl_cft_http3, ctx);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n  cf->conn = conn;\n\n  result = Curl_cf_udp_create(&cf->next, data, conn, ai, TRNSPRT_QUIC);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n  cf->next->conn = cf->conn;\n  cf->next->sockindex = cf->sockindex;\n\nout:\n  *pcf = (!result) ? cf : NULL;\n  if(result) {\n    if(cf)\n      Curl_conn_cf_discard_chain(&cf, data);\n    else if(ctx)\n      cf_ngtcp2_ctx_free(ctx);\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n#endif\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     goto out;",
        "   }",
        " ",
        "+  /* RFC 9218 Extensible Priorities: set stream priority if configured */",
        "+#ifdef NGHTTP3_PRIORITY_UPDATE_SUPPORTED",
        "+  if(data->set.h3_stream_priority_urgency > 0 ||",
        "+     data->set.h3_stream_priority_incremental) {",
        "+    nghttp3_pri pri;",
        "+    pri.urgency = data->set.h3_stream_priority_urgency;",
        "+    pri.incremental = data->set.h3_stream_priority_incremental ? 1 : 0;",
        "+    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id, &pri);",
        "+    if(rc) {",
        "+      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] failed to set priority -> \"",
        "+                  \"%d (%s)\", stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));",
        "+    }",
        "+    else {",
        "+      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] priority set: urgency=%u, \"",
        "+                  \"incremental=%d\", stream->id,",
        "+                  data->set.h3_stream_priority_urgency,",
        "+                  data->set.h3_stream_priority_incremental);",
        "+    }",
        "+  }",
        "+#endif",
        "+",
        "   cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(cf, data, stream);",
        " ",
        "   if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {"
      ],
      "newLines": 27,

    }

79. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n#include \"unitcheck.h\"\n\n#include \"curl.h\"\n\nstatic CURLcode test_h3_priority_parsing(const char *arg)\n{\n  UNITTEST_BEGIN_SIMPLE\n\n  (void)arg;\n\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  CURL *curl;\n  CURLcode res = CURLE_OK;\n\n  curl = curl_easy_init();\n  if(!curl) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to init curl\");\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  /* Test valid priority format: u=3 */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=3\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set basic urgency\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test valid priority format with incremental: u=5,i */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=5,i\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set urgency with incremental flag\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test edge case: u=0 (highest priority) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=0\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set u=0\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test edge case: u=7 (lowest priority) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=7\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set u=7\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test invalid format: out of range urgency */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=8\");\n  if(res == CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Should have rejected u=8\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n\n  /* Test invalid format: missing u= */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"3\");\n  if(res == CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Should have rejected format without u=\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n\n  curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n\n#endif\n\n  UNITTEST_END_SIMPLE\n}\n\nDECLARE_TEST_UNIT(unit_h3_priority_parsing);\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/unit/unit_h3_priority.c"
}
Output
{
  "content": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n#include \"unitcheck.h\"\n\n#include \"curl.h\"\n\nstatic CURLcode test_h3_priority_parsing(const char *arg)\n{\n  UNITTEST_BEGIN_SIMPLE\n\n  (void)arg;\n\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  CURL *curl;\n  CURLcode res = CURLE_OK;\n\n  curl = curl_easy_init();\n  if(!curl) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to init curl\");\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  /* Test valid priority format: u=3 */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=3\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set basic urgency\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test valid priority format with incremental: u=5,i */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=5,i\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set urgency with incremental flag\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test edge case: u=0 (highest priority) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=0\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set u=0\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test edge case: u=7 (lowest priority) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=7\");\n  if(res != CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Failed to set u=7\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  /* Test invalid format: out of range urgency */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=8\");\n  if(res == CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Should have rejected u=8\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n\n  /* Test invalid format: missing u= */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"3\");\n  if(res == CURLE_OK) {\n    UNITTEST_ERROR(\"Should have rejected format without u=\");\n    curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n\n  curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n\n#endif\n\n  UNITTEST_END_SIMPLE\n}\n\nDECLARE_TEST_UNIT(unit_h3_priority_parsing);\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/tests/unit/unit_h3_priority.c",
  "originalFile": null,

87. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/setopt.c",
  "limit": 60,
  "offset": 2298
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  case CURLOPT_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS:\n    /*\n     * Set accepted signature algorithms.\n     * Specify colon-delimited list of signature scheme names.\n     */\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS))\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS], ptr);\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY:\n    /*\n     * Set HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218)\n     * Format: \"u=N[,i]\" where N is urgency (0-7) and i is incremental flag\n     */\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n    if(ptr) {\n      const char *p = ptr;\n      unsigned long urgency = 0;\n      bool incremental = FALSE;\n\n      /* Parse urgency: u=N */\n      if(p[0] == 'u' && p[1] == '=') {\n        char *endp = NULL;\n        p += 2;\n        urgency = strtoul(p, &endp, 10);\n        if(urgency > 7)\n          return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n        p = endp;\n\n        /* Check for incremental flag: ,i */\n        if(p && *p == ',') {\n          p++;\n          if(*p == 'i') {\n            incremental = TRUE;\n            p++;\n          }\n        }\n        /* Ensure string ends properly */\n        if(p && *p != '\\0')\n          return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n        s->h3_stream_priority_urgency = (uint8_t)urgency;\n        s->h3_stream_priority_incremental = incremental;\n      }\n      else\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    }\n    else {\n      /* Reset to defaults */\n      s->h3_stream_priority_urgency = 3;\n      s->h3_stream_priority_incremental = FALSE;\n    }\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:\n    /*\n     * Set pinned public key for SSL connection.\n     * Specify filename of the public key in DER format.\n     */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/setopt.c",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 2298,
    "totalLines": 3031
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{

  "limit": 10,

}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  {\"http3\",                      ARG_NONE|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_HTTP3},\n  {\"http3-only\",                 ARG_NONE|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_HTTP3_ONLY},\n  {\"h3-priority\",                ARG_STRG, ' ', C_H3_PRIORITY},\n  {\"ignore-content-length\",      ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_IGNORE_CONTENT_LENGTH},\n  {\"include\",                    ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_INCLUDE},\n  {\"insecure\",                   ARG_BOOL, 'k', C_INSECURE},\n  {\"interface\",                  ARG_STRG, ' ', C_INTERFACE},\n  {\"ip-tos\",                     ARG_STRG, ' ', C_IP_TOS},\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_IPFS\n  {\"ipfs-gateway\",               ARG_STRG, ' ', C_IPFS_GATEWAY},",

  "file": {
    "content": "  case C_H3_PRIORITY: /* --h3-priority */\n    /* Set HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218)\n     * Format: \"u=N[,i]\" where N is urgency (0-7) and i is incremental flag\n     */\n    if(!feature_http3)\n      return PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;\n    config->h3_priority = nextarg;\n    break;\n  case C_TLSV1: /* --tlsv1 */\n    err = opt_sslver(config, 1);\n    break;\n  case C_TLSV1_0: /* --tlsv1.0 */\n    err = opt_sslver(config, 1);\n    break;\n  case C_TLSV1_1: /* --tlsv1.1 */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/src/tool_getparam.c",
    "numLines": 15,
    "startLine": 1790,
    "totalLines": 3159
  },
  "type": "text"
}

    "content": "    MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_SSL_EC_CURVES, config->ssl_ec_curves);\n\n  if(config->ssl_signature_algorithms)\n    MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS,\n                  config->ssl_signature_algorithms);\n\n  if(config->h3_priority)\n    MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY,\n                  config->h3_priority);\n\n  if(config->writeout)\n    my_setopt_long(curl, CURLOPT_CERTINFO, 1);\n\n  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_SSLCERT, config->cert);\n  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERT, config->proxy_cert);\n  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_SSLCERTTYPE, config->cert_type);\n  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERTTYPE, config->proxy_cert_type);\n  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_SSLKEY, config->key);\n  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEY, config->proxy_key);\n  MY_SETOPT_STR(curl, CURLOPT_SSLKEYTYPE, config->key_type);",

93. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c",
  "limit": 60,
  "offset": 1668
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_request(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,\n                                   nva, nheader, preader, data);\n  if(rc) {\n    switch(rc) {\n    case NGHTTP3_ERR_CONN_CLOSING:\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] failed to send, \"\n                  \"connection is closing\", stream->id);\n      break;\n    default:\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] failed to send -> \"\n                  \"%d (%s)\", stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));\n      break;\n    }\n    cf_ngtcp2_stream_close(cf, data, stream);\n    result = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  /* RFC 9218 Extensible Priorities: set stream priority if configured */\n#ifdef NGHTTP3_PRIORITY_UPDATE_SUPPORTED\n  if(data->set.h3_stream_priority_urgency > 0 ||\n     data->set.h3_stream_priority_incremental) {\n    nghttp3_pri pri;\n    pri.urgency = data->set.h3_stream_priority_urgency;\n    pri.incremental = data->set.h3_stream_priority_incremental ? 1 : 0;\n    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id, &pri);\n    if(rc) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] failed to set priority -> \"\n                  \"%d (%s)\", stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));\n    }\n    else {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\" PRId64 \"] priority set: urgency=%u, \"\n                  \"incremental=%d\", stream->id,\n                  data->set.h3_stream_priority_urgency,\n                  data->set.h3_stream_priority_incremental);\n    }\n  }\n#endif\n\n  cf_ngtcp2_upd_rx_win(cf, data, stream);\n\n  if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {\n    infof(data, \"[HTTP/3] [%\" PRId64 \"] OPENED stream for %s\",\n          stream->id, Curl_bufref_ptr(&data->state.url));\n    for(i = 0; i < nheader; ++i) {\n      infof(data, \"[HTTP/3] [%\" PRId64 \"] [%.*s: %.*s]\", stream->id,\n            (int)nva[i].namelen, nva[i].name,\n            (int)nva[i].valuelen, nva[i].value);\n    }\n  }\n\nout:\n  curlx_free(nva);\n  Curl_dynhds_free(&h2_headers);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_send(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               const uint8_t *buf, size_t len, bool eos,\n                               size_t *pnwritten)",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1668,
    "totalLines": 2974
  },
  "type": "text"
}

97. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/easy.c",
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 1095
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  Curl_async_shutdown(data);\n  Curl_resolv_unlink(data, &data->state.dns[0]);\n  Curl_resolv_unlink(data, &data->state.dns[1]);\n  /* zero out UserDefined data: */\n  Curl_freeset(data);\n  memset(&data->set, 0, sizeof(struct UserDefined));\n  Curl_init_userdefined(data);\n\n  /* zero out Progress data: */\n  memset(&data->progress, 0, sizeof(struct Progress));\n\n  /* zero out PureInfo data: */\n  Curl_initinfo(data);\n\n  data->progress.hide = TRUE;\n  data->state.current_speed = -1; /* init to negative == impossible */\n  data->state.recent_conn_id = -1; /* clear remembered connection id */\n\n  /* zero out authentication data: */\n  memset(&data->state.authhost, 0, sizeof(struct auth));\n  memset(&data->state.authproxy, 0, sizeof(struct auth));\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH)\n  Curl_http_auth_cleanup_digest(data);\n#endif\n  data->master_mid = UINT32_MAX;\n}\n\n/*\n * curl_easy_pause() allows an application to pause or unpause a specific\n * transfer and direction. This function sets the full new state for the\n * current connection this easy handle operates on.\n *\n * NOTE: if you have the receiving paused and you call this function to remove\n * the pausing, you may get your write callback called at this point.\n *\n * Action is a bitmask consisting of CURLPAUSE_* bits in curl/curl.h\n *\n * NOTE: This is one of few API functions that are allowed to be called from\n * within a callback.\n */\nCURLcode curl_easy_pause(CURL *d, int action)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  bool recursive = FALSE;\n  bool changed = FALSE;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = d;\n  bool recv_paused, recv_paused_new;\n  bool send_paused, send_paused_new;\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/easy.c",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1095,
    "totalLines": 1398
  },
  "type": "text"
}

100. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/url.c",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 337
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "void Curl_init_userdefined(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *set = &data->set;\n\n  set->out = stdout;  /* default output to stdout */\n  set->in_set = stdin;  /* default input from stdin */\n  set->err = stderr;  /* default stderr to stderr */\n\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic push\n#pragma clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wcast-function-type-strict\"\n#endif\n  /* use fwrite as default function to store output */\n  set->fwrite_func = (curl_write_callback)fwrite;\n\n  /* use fread as default function to read input */\n  set->fread_func_set = (curl_read_callback)fread;\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic pop\n#endif\n  set->is_fread_set = 0;\n\n  set->seek_client = ZERO_NULL;\n\n  set->filesize = -1;        /* we do not know the size */\n  set->postfieldsize = -1;   /* unknown size */\n  set->maxredirs = 30;       /* sensible default */\n\n  set->method = HTTPREQ_GET; /* Default HTTP request */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  set->rtspreq = RTSPREQ_OPTIONS; /* Default RTSP request */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  set->ftp_use_epsv = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPSV operations */\n  set->ftp_use_eprt = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPRT operations */\n  set->ftp_use_pret = FALSE;  /* mainly useful for drftpd servers */\n  set->ftp_filemethod = FTPFILE_MULTICWD;\n  set->ftp_skip_ip = TRUE;    /* skip PASV IP by default */\n#endif\n  set->dns_cache_timeout_ms = 60000; /* Timeout every 60 seconds by default */\n\n  /* Timeout every 24 hours by default */\n  set->general_ssl.ca_cache_timeout = 24 * 60 * 60;\n\n  set->httpauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC;  /* defaults to basic */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  set->proxyport = 0;\n  set->proxytype = CURLPROXY_HTTP; /* defaults to HTTP proxy */\n  set->proxyauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC; /* defaults to basic */\n  /* SOCKS5 proxy auth defaults to username/password + GSS-API */\n  set->socks5auth = CURLAUTH_BASIC | CURLAUTH_GSSAPI;\n#endif\n\n  Curl_ssl_easy_config_init(data);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  set->doh_verifyhost = TRUE;\n  set->doh_verifypeer = TRUE;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  /* defaults to any auth type */\n  set->ssh_auth_types = CURLSSH_AUTH_DEFAULT;\n  set->new_directory_perms = 0755; /* Default permissions */\n#endif\n\n  set->new_file_perms = 0644;    /* Default permissions */\n  set->allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t) CURLPROTO_ALL;\n  set->redir_protocols = CURLPROTO_REDIR;\n\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  /*\n   * disallow unprotected protection negotiation NEC reference implementation\n   * seem not to follow rfc1961 section 4.3/4.4\n   */\n  set->socks5_gssapi_nec = FALSE;\n#endif\n\n  /* set default minimum TLS version */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  Curl_setopt_SSLVERSION(data, CURLOPT_SSLVERSION, CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  Curl_setopt_SSLVERSION(data, CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLVERSION,\n                         CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT);\n#endif\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  set->wildcard_enabled = FALSE;\n  set->chunk_bgn = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->chunk_end = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->fnmatch = ZERO_NULL;\n#endif\n  set->tcp_keepalive = FALSE;\n  set->tcp_keepintvl = 60;\n  set->tcp_keepidle = 60;\n  set->tcp_keepcnt = 9;\n  set->tcp_fastopen = FALSE;\n  set->tcp_nodelay = TRUE;\n  set->ssl_enable_alpn = TRUE;\n  set->expect_100_timeout = 1000L; /* Wait for a second by default. */\n  set->sep_headers = TRUE; /* separated header lists by default */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/url.c",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 337,
    "totalLines": 3904
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Cannot use a 0 assert here since compilers\n   * are not in agreement if they then want a NORETURN attribute or\n   * not. *sigh* */\n  DEBUGASSERT(p == NULL);\n}\n\n/**\n * Curl_open()\n *",

\"url.h\"\n#include \"connect.h\"\n#include \"curl_rtmp.h\"\n#include \"gopher.h\"\n#include \"mqtt.h\"\n#include \"rtsp.h\"\n#include \"smtp.h\"\n#include \"ws.h\"\n\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\nstatic void data_priority_cleanup(struct Curl_easy *data);\n#else\n#define data_priority_cleanup(x)\n#endif\n\n/* Some parts of the code (e.g. chunked encoding) assume this buffer has at\n * more than just a few bytes to play with. Do not let it become too small or\n * bad things will happen.\n */\n#if READBUFFER_SIZE < READBUFFER_MIN\n# error READBUFFER_SIZE is too small\n#endif\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_PROXY) && defined(USE_UNIX_SOCKETS)\n#define UNIX_SOCKET_PREFIX \"localhost\"\n#endif\n\n/* Reject URLs exceeding this length */\n#define MAX_URL_LEN 0xffff\n\n/*\n * get_protocol_family()\n *\n * This is used to return the protocol family for a given protocol.\n *\n * Parameters:\n *\n * 's'  [in]  - struct Curl_scheme pointer.\n *\n * Returns the family as a single bit protocol identifier.\n */\nstatic curl_prot_t get_protocol_family(const struct Curl_scheme *s)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(s);\n  DEBUGASSERT(s->family);\n  return s->family;\n}\n\nvoid Curl_freeset(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  /* Free all dynamic strings stored in the data->set substructure. */\n  enum dupstring i;\n  enum dupblob j;\n\n  for(i = (enum dupstring)0; i < STRING_LAST; i++) {\n    Curl_safefree(data->set.str[i]);\n  }\n\n  for(j = (enum dupblob)0; j < BLOB_LAST; j++) {\n    Curl_safefree(data->set.blobs[j]);\n  }\n\n  Curl_bufref_free(&data->state.referer);\n  Curl_bufref_free(&data->state.url);\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MIME) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API)\n  Curl_mime_cleanpart(data->set.mimepostp);\n  Curl_safefree(data->set.mimepostp);\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  curl_slist_free_all(data->state.cookielist);\n  data->state.cookielist = NULL;\n#endif\n}\n\n/* free the URL pieces */\nstatic void up_free(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct urlpieces *up = &data->state.up;\n  Curl_safefree(up->scheme);\n  Curl_safefree(up->hostname);\n  Curl_safefree(up->port);\n  Curl_safefree(up->user);\n  Curl_safefree(up->password);\n  Curl_safefree(up->options);\n  Curl_safefree(up->path);\n  Curl_safefree(up->query);\n  curl_url_cleanup(data->state.uh);\n  data->state.uh = NULL;\n}\n\n/*\n * This is the internal function curl_easy_cleanup() calls. This should\n * cleanup and free all resources associated with this sessionhandle.\n *\n * We ignore SIGPIPE when this is called from curl_easy_cleanup.\n */\n\nCURLcode Curl_close(struct Curl_easy **datap)\n{\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n\n  if(!datap || !*datap)\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  data = *datap;\n  *datap = NULL;\n\n  if(!data->state.internal && data->multi) {\n    /* This handle is still part of a multi handle, take care of this first\n       and detach this handle from there.\n       This detaches the connection. */\n    curl_multi_remove_handle(data->multi, data);\n  }\n  else {\n    /* Detach connection if any is left. This should not be normal, but can be\n       the case for example with CONNECT_ONLY + recv/send (test 556) */\n    Curl_detach_connection(data);\n    if(!data->state.internal && data->multi_easy) {\n      /* when curl_easy_perform() is used, it creates its own multi handle to\n         use and this is the one */\n      curl_multi_cleanup(data->multi_easy);\n      data->multi_easy = NULL;\n    }\n  }\n  DEBUGASSERT(!data->conn || data->state.internal);\n\n  Curl_expire_clear(data); /* shut off any timers left */\n\n  if(data->state.rangestringalloc)\n    curlx_free(data->state.range);\n\n  /* release any resolve information this transfer kept */\n  Curl_async_destroy(data);\n  Curl_resolv_unlink(data, &data->state.dns[0]); /* done with this */\n  Curl_resolv_unlink(data, &data->state.dns[1]);\n\n  data->set.verbose = FALSE; /* no more calls to DEBUGFUNCTION */\n  data->magic = 0; /* force a clear AFTER the possibly enforced removal from\n                    * the multi handle and async dns shutdown. The multi\n                    * handle might check the magic and so might any\n                    * DEBUGFUNCTION invoked for tracing */\n\n  /* freed here just in case DONE was not called */\n  Curl_req_free(&data->req, data);\n\n  /* Close down all open SSL info and sessions */\n  Curl_ssl_close_all(data);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.first_host);\n  Curl_ssl_free_certinfo(data);\n\n  Curl_bufref_free(&data->state.referer);\n\n  up_free(data);\n  curlx_dyn_free(&data->state.headerb);\n  Curl_flush_cookies(data, TRUE);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC\n  Curl_altsvc_save(data, data->asi, data->set.str[STRING_ALTSVC]);\n  Curl_altsvc_cleanup(&data->asi);\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  Curl_hsts_save(data, data->hsts, data->set.str[STRING_HSTS]);\n  if(!data->share || !data->share->hsts)\n    Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n  curl_slist_free_all(data->state.hstslist); /* clean up list */\n#endif\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH)\n  Curl_http_auth_cleanup_digest(data);\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.most_recent_ftp_entrypath);\n  Curl_safefree(data->info.contenttype);\n  Curl_safefree(data->info.wouldredirect);\n\n  data_priority_cleanup(data);\n\n  /* No longer a dirty share, if it exists */\n  if(data->share) {\n    Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n    data->share->dirty--;\n    Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n  }\n\n  Curl_hash_destroy(&data->meta_hash);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.proxyuserpwd);\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.uagent);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.userpwd);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.accept_encoding);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.rangeline);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.ref);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.host);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.cookiehost);\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.rtsp_transport);\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.user);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.passwd);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.proxyuser);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.proxypasswd);\n#endif\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API)\n  Curl_mime_cleanpart(data->state.formp);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.formp);\n#endif\n\n  /* destruct wildcard structures if it is needed */\n  Curl_wildcard_dtor(&data->wildcard);\n  Curl_freeset(data);\n  Curl_headers_cleanup(data);\n  Curl_netrc_cleanup(&data->state.netrc);\n  curlx_free(data);\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * Initialize the UserDefined fields within a Curl_easy.\n * This may be safely called on a new or existing Curl_easy.\n */\nvoid Curl_init_userdefined(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *set = &data->set;\n\n  set->out = stdout;  /* default output to stdout */\n  set->in_set = stdin;  /* default input from stdin */\n  set->err = stderr;  /* default stderr to stderr */\n\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic push\n#pragma clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wcast-function-type-strict\"\n#endif\n  /* use fwrite as default function to store output */\n  set->fwrite_func = (curl_write_callback)fwrite;\n\n  /* use fread as default function to read input */\n  set->fread_func_set = (curl_read_callback)fread;\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic pop\n#endif\n  set->is_fread_set = 0;\n\n  set->seek_client = ZERO_NULL;\n\n  set->filesize = -1;        /* we do not know the size */\n  set->postfieldsize = -1;   /* unknown size */\n  set->maxredirs = 30;       /* sensible default */\n\n  set->method = HTTPREQ_GET; /* Default HTTP request */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  set->rtspreq = RTSPREQ_OPTIONS; /* Default RTSP request */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  set->ftp_use_epsv = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPSV operations */\n  set->ftp_use_eprt = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPRT operations */\n  set->ftp_use_pret = FALSE;  /* mainly useful for drftpd servers */\n  set->ftp_filemethod = FTPFILE_MULTICWD;\n  set->ftp_skip_ip = TRUE;    /* skip PASV IP by default */\n#endif\n  set->dns_cache_timeout_ms = 60000; /* Timeout every 60 seconds by default */\n\n  /* Timeout every 24 hours by default */\n  set->general_ssl.ca_cache_timeout = 24 * 60 * 60;\n\n  set->httpauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC;  /* defaults to basic */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  set->proxyport = 0;\n  set->proxytype = CURLPROXY_HTTP; /* defaults to HTTP proxy */\n  set->proxyauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC; /* defaults to basic */\n  /* SOCKS5 proxy auth defaults to username/password + GSS-API */\n  set->socks5auth = CURLAUTH_BASIC | CURLAUTH_GSSAPI;\n#endif\n\n  Curl_ssl_easy_config_init(data);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  set->doh_verifyhost = TRUE;\n  set->doh_verifypeer = TRUE;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  /* defaults to any auth type */\n  set->ssh_auth_types = CURLSSH_AUTH_DEFAULT;\n  set->new_directory_perms = 0755; /* Default permissions */\n#endif\n\n  set->new_file_perms = 0644;    /* Default permissions */\n  set->allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t) CURLPROTO_ALL;\n  set->redir_protocols = CURLPROTO_REDIR;\n\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  /*\n   * disallow unprotected protection negotiation NEC reference implementation\n   * seem not to follow rfc1961 section 4.3/4.4\n   */\n  set->socks5_gssapi_nec = FALSE;\n#endif\n\n  /* set default minimum TLS version */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  Curl_setopt_SSLVERSION(data, CURLOPT_SSLVERSION, CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  Curl_setopt_SSLVERSION(data, CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLVERSION,\n                         CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT);\n#endif\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  set->wildcard_enabled = FALSE;\n  set->chunk_bgn = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->chunk_end = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->fnmatch = ZERO_NULL;\n#endif\n  set->tcp_keepalive = FALSE;\n  set->tcp_keepintvl = 60;\n  set->tcp_keepidle = 60;\n  set->tcp_keepcnt = 9;\n  set->tcp_fastopen = FALSE;\n  set->tcp_nodelay = TRUE;\n  set->ssl_enable_alpn = TRUE;\n  set->expect_100_timeout = 1000L; /* Wait for a second by default. */\n  set->sep_headers = TRUE; /* separated header lists by default */\n  set->buffer_size = READBUFFER_SIZE;\n  set->upload_buffer_size = UPLOADBUFFER_DEFAULT;\n  set->happy_eyeballs_timeout = CURL_HET_DEFAULT;\n  set->upkeep_interval_ms = CURL_UPKEEP_INTERVAL_DEFAULT;\n  set->maxconnects = DEFAULT_CONNCACHE_SIZE; /* for easy handles */\n  set->conn_max_idle_ms = 118 * 1000;\n  set->conn_max_age_ms = 24 * 3600 * 1000;\n  set->http09_allowed = FALSE;\n  set->httpwant = CURL_HTTP_VERSION_NONE;\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  memset(&set->priority, 0, sizeof(set->priority));\n#endif\n  set->quick_exit = 0L;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_WEBSOCKETS\n  set->ws_raw_mode = FALSE;\n  set->ws_no_auto_pong = FALSE;\n#endif\n}\n\n/* easy->meta_hash destructor. Should never be called as elements\n * MUST be added with their own destructor */\nstatic void easy_meta_freeentry(void *p)\n{\n  (void)p;\n  /* Will always be FALSE. Cannot use a 0 assert here since compilers\n   * are not in agreement if they then want a NORETURN attribute or\n   * not. *sigh* */\n  DEBUGASSERT(p == NULL);\n}\n\n/**\n * Curl_open()\n *\n * @param curl is a pointer to a sessionhandle pointer that gets set by this\n * function.\n * @return CURLcode\n */\n\nCURLcode Curl_open(struct Curl_easy **curl)\n{\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n\n  /* simple start-up: alloc the struct, init it with zeroes and return */\n  data = curlx_calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_easy));\n  if(!data) {\n    /* this is a serious error */\n    DEBUGF(curl_mfprintf(stderr, \"Error: calloc of Curl_easy failed\\n\"));\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  data->magic = CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER;\n  /* most recent connection is not yet defined */\n  data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\n  data->state.recent_conn_id = -1;\n  /* and not assigned an id yet */\n  data->id = -1;\n  data->mid = UINT32_MAX;\n  data->master_mid = UINT32_MAX;\n  data->progress.hide = TRUE;\n  data->state.current_speed = -1; /* init to negative == impossible */\n\n  Curl_hash_init(&data->meta_hash, 23,\n                 Curl_hash_str, curlx_str_key_compare, easy_meta_freeentry);\n  curlx_dyn_init(&data->state.headerb, CURL_MAX_HTTP_HEADER);\n  Curl_bufref_init(&data->state.url);\n  Curl_bufref_init(&data->state.referer);\n  Curl_req_init(&data->req);\n  Curl_initinfo(data);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  Curl_llist_init(&data->state.httphdrs, NULL);\n#endif\n  Curl_netrc_init(&data->state.netrc);\n  Curl_init_userdefined(data);\n\n  *curl = data;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nvoid Curl_conn_free(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn)\n{\n  size_t i;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(conn);\n\n  if(conn->scheme && conn->scheme->run->disconnect &&\n     !conn->bits.shutdown_handler)\n    conn->scheme->run->disconnect(data, conn, TRUE);\n\n  for(i = 0; i < CURL_ARRAYSIZE(conn->cfilter); ++i) {\n    Curl_conn_cf_discard_all(data, conn, (int)i);\n  }\n\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->host);\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->conn_to_host);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->http_proxy.host);\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->socks_proxy.host);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->http_proxy.user);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->socks_proxy.user);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->http_proxy.passwd);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->socks_proxy.passwd);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->http_proxy.host.rawalloc); /* http proxy name buffer */\n  Curl_safefree(conn->socks_proxy.host.rawalloc); /* socks proxy name buffer */\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(conn->user);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->passwd);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->sasl_authzid);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->options);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->oauth_bearer);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->host.rawalloc); /* hostname buffer */\n  Curl_safefree(conn->conn_to_host.rawalloc); /* hostname buffer */\n  Curl_safefree(conn->hostname_resolve);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->secondaryhostname);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->localdev);\n  Curl_ssl_conn_config_cleanup(conn);\n\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  Curl_safefree(conn->unix_domain_socket);\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(conn->destination);\n  Curl_hash_destroy(&conn->meta_hash);\n\n  curlx_free(conn); /* free all the connection oriented data */\n}\n\n/*\n * xfer_may_multiplex()\n *\n * Return a TRUE, iff the transfer can be done over an (appropriate)\n * multiplexed connection.\n */\nstatic bool xfer_may_multiplex(const struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               const struct connectdata *conn)\n{\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  /* If an HTTP protocol and multiplexing is enabled */\n  if((conn->scheme->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP) &&\n     (!conn->bits.protoconnstart || !conn->bits.close)) {\n\n    if(Curl_multiplex_wanted(data->multi) &&\n       (data->state.http_neg.allowed & (CURL_HTTP_V2x | CURL_HTTP_V3x)))\n      /* allows HTTP/2 or newer */\n      return TRUE;\n  }\n#else\n  (void)data;\n  (void)conn;\n#endif\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\nstatic bool proxy_info_matches(const struct proxy_info *data,\n                               const struct proxy_info *needle)\n{\n  if((data->proxytype == needle->proxytype) &&\n     (data->port == needle->port) &&\n     curl_strequal(data->host.name, needle->host.name))\n    return TRUE;\n\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\nstatic bool socks_proxy_info_matches(const struct proxy_info *data,\n                                     const struct proxy_info *needle)\n{\n  if(!proxy_info_matches(data, needle))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  /* the user information is case-sensitive\n     or at least it is not defined as case-insensitive\n     see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3986#section-3.2.1 */\n\n  /* curl_strequal does a case insensitive comparison,\n     so do not use it here! */\n  if(Curl_timestrcmp(data->user, needle->user) ||\n     Curl_timestrcmp(data->passwd, needle->passwd))\n    return FALSE;\n  return TRUE;\n}\n#endif\n\n/* A connection has to have been idle for less than 'conn_max_idle_ms'\n   (the success rate is just too low after this), or created less than\n   'conn_max_age_ms' ago, to be subject for reuse. */\nstatic bool conn_maxage(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                        struct connectdata *conn,\n                        struct curltime now)\n{\n  timediff_t age_ms;\n\n  if(data->set.conn_max_idle_ms) {\n    age_ms = curlx_ptimediff_ms(&now, &conn->lastused);\n    if(age_ms > data->set.conn_max_idle_ms) {\n      infof(data, \"Too old connection (%\" FMT_TIMEDIFF_T\n            \" ms idle, max idle is %\" FMT_TIMEDIFF_T \" ms), disconnect it\",\n            age_ms, data->set.conn_max_idle_ms);\n      return TRUE;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(data->set.conn_max_age_ms) {\n    age_ms = curlx_ptimediff_ms(&now, &conn->created);\n    if(age_ms > data->set.conn_max_age_ms) {\n      infof(data,\n            \"Too old connection (created %\" FMT_TIMEDIFF_T\n            \" ms ago, max lifetime is %\" FMT_TIMEDIFF_T \" ms), disconnect it\",\n            age_ms, data->set.conn_max_age_ms);\n      return TRUE;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n/*\n * Return TRUE iff the given connection is considered dead.\n */\nbool Curl_conn_seems_dead(struct connectdata *conn,\n                          struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(!data->conn);\n  if(!CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\n    /* The check for a dead socket makes sense only if the connection is not in\n       use */\n    bool dead;\n\n    if(conn_maxage(data, conn, *Curl_pgrs_now(data))) {\n      /* avoid check if already too old */\n      dead = TRUE;\n    }\n    else if(conn->scheme->run->connection_check) {\n      /* The protocol has a special method for checking the state of the\n         connection. Use it to check if the connection is dead. */\n      unsigned int state;\n\n      /* briefly attach the connection to this transfer for the purpose of\n         checking it */\n      Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n\n      state = conn->scheme->run->connection_check(data, conn,\n                                                     CONNCHECK_ISDEAD);\n      dead = (state & CONNRESULT_DEAD);\n      /* detach the connection again */\n      Curl_detach_connection(data);\n    }\n    else {\n      bool input_pending = FALSE;\n\n      Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n      dead = !Curl_conn_is_alive(data, conn, &input_pending);\n      if(input_pending) {\n        /* For reuse, we want a \"clean\" connection state. The includes\n         * that we expect - in general - no waiting input data. Input\n         * waiting might be a TLS Notify Close, for example. We reject\n         * that.\n         * For protocols where data from other end may arrive at\n         * any time (HTTP/2 PING for example), the protocol handler needs\n         * to install its own `connection_check` callback.\n         */\n        DEBUGF(infof(data, \"connection has input pending, not reusable\"));\n        dead = TRUE;\n      }\n      Curl_detach_connection(data);\n    }\n\n    if(dead) {\n      /* remove connection from cpool */\n      infof(data, \"Connection %\" FMT_OFF_T \" seems to be dead\",\n            conn->connection_id);\n      return TRUE;\n    }\n  }\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\nCURLcode Curl_conn_upkeep(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                          struct connectdata *conn)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  if(curlx_ptimediff_ms(Curl_pgrs_now(data), &conn->keepalive) <=\n     data->set.upkeep_interval_ms)\n    return result;\n\n  /* briefly attach for action */\n  Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n  if(conn->scheme->run->connection_check) {\n    /* Do a protocol-specific keepalive check on the connection. */\n    unsigned int rc;\n    rc = conn->scheme->run->connection_check(data, conn,\n                                                CONNCHECK_KEEPALIVE);\n    if(rc & CONNRESULT_DEAD)\n      result = CURLE_RECV_ERROR;\n  }\n  else {\n    /* Do the generic action on the FIRSTSOCKET filter chain */\n    result = Curl_conn_keep_alive(data, conn, FIRSTSOCKET);\n  }\n  Curl_detach_connection(data);\n\n  conn->keepalive = *Curl_pgrs_now(data);\n  return result;\n}\n\n#ifdef USE_SSH\nstatic bool ssh_config_matches(struct connectdata *one,\n                               struct connectdata *two)\n{\n  struct ssh_conn *sshc1, *sshc2;\n\n  sshc1 = Curl_conn_meta_get(one, CURL_META_SSH_CONN);\n  sshc2 = Curl_conn_meta_get(two, CURL_META_SSH_CONN);\n  return sshc1 && sshc2 && Curl_safecmp(sshc1->rsa, sshc2->rsa) &&\n         Curl_safecmp(sshc1->rsa_pub, sshc2->rsa_pub);\n}\n#endif\n\nstruct url_conn_match {\n  struct connectdata *found;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n  struct connectdata *needle;\n  BIT(may_multiplex);\n  BIT(want_ntlm_http);\n  BIT(want_proxy_ntlm_http);\n  BIT(want_nego_http);\n  BIT(want_proxy_nego_http);\n\n  BIT(wait_pipe);\n  BIT(force_reuse);\n  BIT(seen_pending_conn);\n  BIT(seen_single_use_conn);\n  BIT(seen_multiplex_conn);\n};\n\nstatic bool url_match_connect_config(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                     struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  /* connect-only or to-be-closed connections will not be reused */\n  if(conn->connect_only || conn->bits.close || conn->bits.no_reuse)\n    return FALSE;\n\n  /* ip_version must match */\n  if(m->data->set.ipver != CURL_IPRESOLVE_WHATEVER &&\n     m->data->set.ipver != conn->ip_version)\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(m->needle->localdev || m->needle->localport) {\n    /* If we are bound to a specific local end (IP+port), we must not reuse a\n       random other one, although if we did not ask for a particular one we\n       can reuse one that was bound.\n\n       This comparison is a bit rough and too strict. Since the input\n       parameters can be specified in numerous ways and still end up the same\n       it would take a lot of processing to make it really accurate. Instead,\n       this matching will assume that reuses of bound connections will most\n       likely also reuse the exact same binding parameters and missing out a\n       few edge cases should not hurt anyone much.\n    */\n    if((conn->localport != m->needle->localport) ||\n       (conn->localportrange != m->needle->localportrange) ||\n       (m->needle->localdev &&\n        (!conn->localdev || strcmp(conn->localdev, m->needle->localdev))))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n\n  if(m->needle->bits.conn_to_host != conn->bits.conn_to_host)\n    /* do not mix connections that use the \"connect to host\" feature and\n     * connections that do not use this feature */\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(m->needle->bits.conn_to_port != conn->bits.conn_to_port)\n    /* do not mix connections that use the \"connect to port\" feature and\n     * connections that do not use this feature */\n    return FALSE;\n\n  /* Does `conn` use the correct protocol? */\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  if(m->needle->unix_domain_socket) {\n    if(!conn->unix_domain_socket)\n      return FALSE;\n    if(strcmp(m->needle->unix_domain_socket, conn->unix_domain_socket))\n      return FALSE;\n    if(m->needle->bits.abstract_unix_socket != conn->bits.abstract_unix_socket)\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  else if(conn->unix_domain_socket)\n    return FALSE;\n#endif\n\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_fully_connected(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                      struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(!Curl_conn_is_connected(conn, FIRSTSOCKET) ||\n     conn->bits.upgrade_in_progress) {\n    /* Not yet connected, or a protocol upgrade is in progress. The later\n     * happens for HTTP/2 Upgrade: requests that need a response. */\n    if(m->may_multiplex) {\n      m->seen_pending_conn = TRUE;\n      /* Do not pick a connection that has not connected yet */\n      infof(m->data, \"Connection #%\" FMT_OFF_T\n            \" is not open enough, cannot reuse\", conn->connection_id);\n    }\n    /* Do not pick a connection that has not connected yet */\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_multi(struct connectdata *conn,\n                            struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\n    DEBUGASSERT(conn->attached_multi);\n    if(conn->attached_multi != m->data->multi)\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_multiplex_needs(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                      struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\n    if(!conn->bits.multiplex) {\n      /* conn busy and conn cannot take more transfers */\n      m->seen_single_use_conn = TRUE;\n      return FALSE;\n    }\n    m->seen_multiplex_conn = TRUE;\n    if(!m->may_multiplex || !url_match_multi(conn, m))\n      /* conn busy and transfer cannot be multiplexed */\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_multiplex_limits(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                       struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(CONN_INUSE(conn) && m->may_multiplex) {\n    DEBUGASSERT(conn->bits.multiplex);\n    /* If multiplexed, make sure we do not go over concurrency limit */\n    if(conn->attached_xfers >=\n            Curl_multi_max_concurrent_streams(m->data->multi)) {\n      infof(m->data, \"client side MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS reached\"\n            \", skip (%u)\", conn->attached_xfers);\n      return FALSE;\n    }\n    if(conn->attached_xfers >=\n       Curl_conn_get_max_concurrent(m->data, conn, FIRSTSOCKET)) {\n      infof(m->data, \"MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS reached, skip (%u)\",\n            conn->attached_xfers);\n      return FALSE;\n    }\n    /* When not multiplexed, we have a match here! */\n    infof(m->data, \"Multiplexed connection found\");\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_ssl_use(struct connectdata *conn,\n                              struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(m->needle->scheme->flags & PROTOPT_SSL) {\n    /* We are looking for SSL, if `conn` does not do it, not a match. */\n    if(!Curl_conn_is_ssl(conn, FIRSTSOCKET))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  else if(Curl_conn_is_ssl(conn, FIRSTSOCKET)) {\n    /* If the protocol does not allow reuse of SSL connections OR\n       is of another protocol family, not a match. */\n    if(!(m->needle->scheme->flags & PROTOPT_SSL_REUSE) ||\n       (get_protocol_family(conn->scheme) != m->needle->scheme->protocol))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\nstatic bool url_match_proxy_use(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(m->needle->bits.httpproxy != conn->bits.httpproxy ||\n     m->needle->bits.socksproxy != conn->bits.socksproxy)\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(m->needle->bits.socksproxy &&\n     !socks_proxy_info_matches(&m->needle->socks_proxy, &conn->socks_proxy))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(m->needle->bits.httpproxy) {\n    if(m->needle->bits.tunnel_proxy != conn->bits.tunnel_proxy)\n      return FALSE;\n\n    if(!proxy_info_matches(&m->needle->http_proxy, &conn->http_proxy))\n      return FALSE;\n\n    if(IS_HTTPS_PROXY(m->needle->http_proxy.proxytype)) {\n      /* https proxies come in different types, http/1.1, h2, ... */\n      if(m->needle->http_proxy.proxytype != conn->http_proxy.proxytype)\n        return FALSE;\n      /* match SSL config to proxy */\n      if(!Curl_ssl_conn_config_match(m->data, conn, TRUE)) {\n        DEBUGF(infof(m->data,\n                     \"Connection #%\" FMT_OFF_T\n                     \" has different SSL proxy parameters, cannot reuse\",\n                     conn->connection_id));\n        return FALSE;\n      }\n      /* the SSL config to the server, which may apply here is checked\n       * further below */\n    }\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n#else\n#define url_match_proxy_use(c, m) ((void)c, (void)m, TRUE)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\nstatic bool url_match_http_multiplex(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                     struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(m->may_multiplex &&\n     (m->data->state.http_neg.allowed & (CURL_HTTP_V2x | CURL_HTTP_V3x)) &&\n     (m->needle->scheme->protocol & CURLPROTO_HTTP) &&\n     !conn->httpversion_seen) {\n    if(m->data->set.pipewait) {\n      infof(m->data, \"Server upgrade does not support multiplex yet, wait\");\n      m->found = NULL;\n      m->wait_pipe = TRUE;\n      return TRUE; /* stop searching, we want to wait */\n    }\n    infof(m->data, \"Server upgrade cannot be used\");\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_http_version(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                   struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  /* If looking for HTTP and the HTTP versions allowed do not include\n   * the HTTP version of conn, continue looking. */\n  if((m->needle->scheme->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP)) {\n    switch(Curl_conn_http_version(m->data, conn)) {\n    case 30:\n      if(!(m->data->state.http_neg.allowed & CURL_HTTP_V3x)) {\n        DEBUGF(infof(m->data, \"not reusing conn #%\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T\n                     \", we do not want h3\", conn->connection_id));\n        return FALSE;\n      }\n      break;\n    case 20:\n      if(!(m->data->state.http_neg.allowed & CURL_HTTP_V2x)) {\n        DEBUGF(infof(m->data, \"not reusing conn #%\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T\n                     \", we do not want h2\", conn->connection_id));\n        return FALSE;\n      }\n      break;\n    default:\n      if(!(m->data->state.http_neg.allowed & CURL_HTTP_V1x)) {\n        DEBUGF(infof(m->data, \"not reusing conn #%\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T\n                     \", we do not want h1\", conn->connection_id));\n        return FALSE;\n      }\n      break;\n    }\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n#else\n#define url_match_http_multiplex(c, m) ((void)c, (void)m, TRUE)\n#define url_match_http_version(c, m)   ((void)c, (void)m, TRUE)\n#endif\n\nstatic bool url_match_proto_config(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                   struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(!url_match_http_version(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  if(get_protocol_family(m->needle->scheme) & PROTO_FAMILY_SSH) {\n    if(!ssh_config_matches(m->needle, conn))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  else if(get_protocol_family(m->needle->scheme) & PROTO_FAMILY_FTP) {\n    if(!ftp_conns_match(m->needle, conn))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n#endif\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_auth(struct connectdata *conn,\n                           struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  if(!(m->needle->scheme->flags & PROTOPT_CREDSPERREQUEST)) {\n    /* This protocol requires credentials per connection,\n       so verify that we are using the same name and password as well */\n    if(Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->user, conn->user) ||\n       Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->passwd, conn->passwd) ||\n       Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->sasl_authzid, conn->sasl_authzid) ||\n       Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->oauth_bearer, conn->oauth_bearer)) {\n      /* one of them was different */\n      return FALSE;\n    }\n  }\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n  /* GSS delegation differences do not actually affect every connection\n     and auth method, but this check takes precaution before efficiency */\n  if(m->needle->gssapi_delegation != conn->gssapi_delegation)\n    return FALSE;\n#endif\n\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_destination(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                  struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  /* Additional match requirements if talking TLS OR\n   * not talking to an HTTP proxy OR using a tunnel through a proxy */\n  if((m->needle->scheme->flags & PROTOPT_SSL)\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n     || !m->needle->bits.httpproxy || m->needle->bits.tunnel_proxy\n#endif\n    ) {\n    if(!curl_strequal(m->needle->scheme->name, conn->scheme->name)) {\n      /* `needle` and `conn` do not have the same scheme... */\n      if(get_protocol_family(conn->scheme) != m->needle->scheme->protocol) {\n        /* and `conn`s protocol family is not the protocol `needle` wants.\n         * IMAPS would work for IMAP, but no vice versa. */\n        return FALSE;\n      }\n      /* We are in an IMAPS vs IMAP like case. We expect `conn` to have SSL */\n      if(!Curl_conn_is_ssl(conn, FIRSTSOCKET)) {\n        DEBUGF(infof(m->data, \"Connection #%\" FMT_OFF_T\n                     \" has compatible protocol family, but no SSL, no match\",\n                     conn->connection_id));\n        return FALSE;\n      }\n    }\n\n    /* If needle has \"conn_to_*\" set, conn must match this */\n    if((m->needle->bits.conn_to_host && !curl_strequal(\n        m->needle->conn_to_host.name, conn->conn_to_host.name)) ||\n       (m->needle->bits.conn_to_port &&\n        m->needle->conn_to_port != conn->conn_to_port))\n      return FALSE;\n\n    /* hostname and port must match */\n    if(!curl_strequal(m->needle->host.name, conn->host.name) ||\n       m->needle->remote_port != conn->remote_port)\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_ssl_config(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                 struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  /* If talking TLS, conn needs to use the same SSL options. */\n  if((m->needle->scheme->flags & PROTOPT_SSL) &&\n     !Curl_ssl_conn_config_match(m->data, conn, FALSE)) {\n    DEBUGF(infof(m->data, \"Connection #%\" FMT_OFF_T\n                 \" has different SSL parameters, cannot reuse\",\n                 conn->connection_id));\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\n#ifdef USE_NTLM\nstatic bool url_match_auth_ntlm(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  /* If we are looking for an HTTP+NTLM connection, check if this is\n     already authenticating with the right credentials. If not, keep\n     looking so that we can reuse NTLM connections if\n     possible. (Especially we must not reuse the same connection if\n     partway through a handshake!) */\n  if(m->want_ntlm_http) {\n    if(Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->user, conn->user) ||\n       Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->passwd, conn->passwd)) {\n\n      /* we prefer a credential match, but this is at least a connection\n         that can be reused and \"upgraded\" to NTLM */\n      if(conn->http_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_NONE)\n        m->found = conn;\n      return FALSE;\n    }\n  }\n  else if(conn->http_ntlm_state != NTLMSTATE_NONE) {\n    /* Connection is using NTLM auth but we do not want NTLM */\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  /* Same for Proxy NTLM authentication */\n  if(m->want_proxy_ntlm_http) {\n    /* Both conn->http_proxy.user and conn->http_proxy.passwd can be\n     * NULL */\n    if(!conn->http_proxy.user || !conn->http_proxy.passwd)\n      return FALSE;\n\n    if(Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->http_proxy.user,\n                       conn->http_proxy.user) ||\n       Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->http_proxy.passwd,\n                       conn->http_proxy.passwd))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  else if(conn->proxy_ntlm_state != NTLMSTATE_NONE) {\n    /* Proxy connection is using NTLM auth but we do not want NTLM */\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n#endif\n  if(m->want_ntlm_http || m->want_proxy_ntlm_http) {\n    /* Credentials are already checked, we may use this connection.\n     * With NTLM being weird as it is, we MUST use a\n     * connection where it has already been fully negotiated.\n     * If it has not, we keep on looking for a better one. */\n    m->found = conn;\n\n    if((m->want_ntlm_http &&\n       (conn->http_ntlm_state != NTLMSTATE_NONE)) ||\n        (m->want_proxy_ntlm_http &&\n         (conn->proxy_ntlm_state != NTLMSTATE_NONE))) {\n      /* We must use this connection, no other */\n      m->force_reuse = TRUE;\n      return TRUE;\n    }\n    /* Continue look up for a better connection */\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n#else\n#define url_match_auth_ntlm(c, m) ((void)c, (void)m, TRUE)\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_SPNEGO\nstatic bool url_match_auth_nego(struct connectdata *conn,\n                                struct url_conn_match *m)\n{\n  /* If we are looking for an HTTP+Negotiate connection, check if this is\n     already authenticating with the right credentials. If not, keep looking\n     so that we can reuse Negotiate connections if possible. */\n  if(m->want_nego_http) {\n    if(Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->user, conn->user) ||\n       Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->passwd, conn->passwd))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  else if(conn->http_negotiate_state != GSS_AUTHNONE) {\n    /* Connection is using Negotiate auth but we do not want Negotiate */\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  /* Same for Proxy Negotiate authentication */\n  if(m->want_proxy_nego_http) {\n    /* Both conn->http_proxy.user and conn->http_proxy.passwd can be\n     * NULL */\n    if(!conn->http_proxy.user || !conn->http_proxy.passwd)\n      return FALSE;\n\n    if(Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->http_proxy.user,\n                       conn->http_proxy.user) ||\n       Curl_timestrcmp(m->needle->http_proxy.passwd,\n                       conn->http_proxy.passwd))\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  else if(conn->proxy_negotiate_state != GSS_AUTHNONE) {\n    /* Proxy connection is using Negotiate auth but we do not want Negotiate */\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n#endif\n  if(m->want_nego_http || m->want_proxy_nego_http) {\n    /* Credentials are already checked, we may use this connection. We MUST\n     * use a connection where it has already been fully negotiated. If it has\n     * not, we keep on looking for a better one. */\n    m->found = conn;\n    if((m->want_nego_http &&\n        (conn->http_negotiate_state != GSS_AUTHNONE)) ||\n       (m->want_proxy_nego_http &&\n        (conn->proxy_negotiate_state != GSS_AUTHNONE))) {\n      /* We must use this connection, no other */\n      m->force_reuse = TRUE;\n      return TRUE;\n    }\n    return FALSE; /* get another */\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n#else\n#define url_match_auth_nego(c, m) ((void)(c), (void)(m), TRUE)\n#endif\n\nstatic bool url_match_conn(struct connectdata *conn, void *userdata)\n{\n  struct url_conn_match *m = userdata;\n  /* Check if `conn` can be used for transfer `m->data` */\n\n  /* general connect config setting match? */\n  if(!url_match_connect_config(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  /* match for destination and protocol? */\n  if(!url_match_destination(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!url_match_fully_connected(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!url_match_multiplex_needs(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!url_match_ssl_use(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n  if(!url_match_proxy_use(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n  if(!url_match_ssl_config(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!url_match_http_multiplex(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n  else if(m->wait_pipe)\n    /* we decided to wait on PIPELINING */\n    return TRUE;\n\n  if(!url_match_auth(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!url_match_proto_config(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!url_match_auth_ntlm(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n  else if(m->force_reuse)\n    return TRUE;\n\n  if(!url_match_auth_nego(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n  else if(m->force_reuse)\n    return TRUE;\n\n  if(!url_match_multiplex_limits(conn, m))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!CONN_INUSE(conn) && Curl_conn_seems_dead(conn, m->data)) {\n    /* remove and disconnect. */\n    Curl_conn_terminate(m->data, conn, FALSE);\n    return FALSE;\n  }\n\n  /* conn matches our needs. */\n  m->found = conn;\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic bool url_match_result(bool result, void *userdata)\n{\n  struct url_conn_match *match = userdata;\n  (void)result;\n  if(match->found) {\n    /* Attach it now while still under lock, so the connection does\n     * no longer appear idle and can be reaped. */\n    Curl_attach_connection(match->data, match->found);\n    return TRUE;\n  }\n  else if(match->seen_single_use_conn && !match->seen_multiplex_conn) {\n    /* We have seen a single-use, existing connection to the destination and\n     * no multiplexed one. It seems safe to assume that the server does\n     * not support multiplexing. */\n    match->wait_pipe = FALSE;\n  }\n  else if(match->seen_pending_conn && match->data->set.pipewait) {\n    infof(match->data,\n          \"Found pending candidate for reuse and CURLOPT_PIPEWAIT is set\");\n    match->wait_pipe = TRUE;\n  }\n  match->force_reuse = FALSE;\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n/*\n * Given a transfer and a prototype connection (needle),\n * find and attach an existing connection that matches.\n *\n * Return TRUE if an existing connection was attached.\n * `waitpipe` is TRUE if no existing connection matched, but there\n * might be suitable one in the near future (common cause: multiplexing\n * capability has not been determined yet, e.g. ALPN handshake).\n */\nstatic bool url_attach_existing(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                struct connectdata *needle,\n                                bool *waitpipe)\n{\n  struct url_conn_match match;\n  bool result;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(!data->conn);\n  memset(&match, 0, sizeof(match));\n  match.data = data;\n  match.needle = needle;\n  match.may_multiplex = xfer_may_multiplex(data, needle);\n\n#ifdef USE_NTLM\n  match.want_ntlm_http =\n    (data->state.authhost.want & CURLAUTH_NTLM) &&\n    (needle->scheme->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  match.want_proxy_ntlm_http =\n    needle->bits.proxy_user_passwd &&\n    (data->state.authproxy.want & CURLAUTH_NTLM) &&\n    (needle->scheme->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP);\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && defined(USE_SPNEGO)\n  match.want_nego_http =\n    (data->state.authhost.want & CURLAUTH_NEGOTIATE) &&\n    (needle->scheme->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  match.want_proxy_nego_http =\n    needle->bits.proxy_user_passwd &&\n    (data->state.authproxy.want & CURLAUTH_NEGOTIATE) &&\n    (needle->scheme->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP);\n#endif\n#endif\n\n  /* Find a connection in the pool that matches what \"data + needle\"\n   * requires. If a suitable candidate is found, it is attached to \"data\". */\n  result = Curl_cpool_find(data, needle->destination,\n                           url_match_conn, url_match_result, &match);\n\n  /* wait_pipe is TRUE if we encounter a bundle that is undecided. There\n   * is no matching connection then, yet. */\n  *waitpipe = (bool)match.wait_pipe;\n  return result;\n}\n\n/*\n * Allocate and initialize a new connectdata object.\n */\nstatic struct connectdata *allocate_conn(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct connectdata *conn = curlx_calloc(1, sizeof(struct connectdata));\n  if(!conn)\n    return NULL;\n\n  /* and we setup a few fields in case we end up actually using this struct */\n\n  conn->sock[FIRSTSOCKET] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;     /* no file descriptor */\n  conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD; /* no file descriptor */\n  conn->recv_idx = 0; /* default for receiving transfer data */\n  conn->send_idx = 0; /* default for sending transfer data */\n  conn->connection_id = -1;
